children.name: Sepia
children.description: Sepia — a genus of cuttlefish in the family Sepiidae.

children.name: Sepia acuminata
children.description: Sepia acuminata is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, from Port Elizabeth, South Africa to Somalia (01º30'N–30ºS), and Madagascar.

children.name: Sepia adami
children.description: Sepia adami is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean.

children.name: Sepia appelloefi
children.description: Sepia appelloefi is a species of cuttlefish native to the northwestern Pacific Ocean, specifically the Tsushima Strait and waters between Kyūshū and southern Honshū, Japan.

children.name: Sepia aureomaculata
children.description: Sepia aureomaculata is a species of cuttlefish native to the northwestern Pacific Ocean, specifically the Kii Channel, Tosa Bay, Hyuga-Nada, and the East China Sea.

children.name: Sepia bathyalis
children.description: Sepia bathyalis is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, specifically northwestern and southwestern Madagascar.

children.name: Sepia bertheloti
children.description: Sepia bertheloti, the African cuttlefish, is a species of cuttlefish from the family Sepiidae which is found in the warmer waters of the eastern Atlantic Ocean off Africa.

children.name: Sepia bidhaia
children.description: Sepia bidhaia is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Pacific Ocean, specifically the waters off the Great Barrier Reef ( to ).

children.name: Sepia brevimana
children.description: Sepia brevimana — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia burnupi
children.description: Sepia burnupi is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, specifically southeast Africa, from Port Elizabeth to southern Mozambique and the Saya-de-Malha Bank.

children.name: Sepia carinata
children.description: Sepia carinata is a species of cuttlefish native to the western Pacific Ocean, specifically southern Japan, Sagami Bay, the South China Sea, and Vietnam.

children.name: Sepia chirotrema
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Sepia | parent = Sepia (Sepia) | species = chirotrema | authority = Berry, 1918 | synonyms = | synonyms_ref = }}.

children.name: Sepia confusa
children.description: Sepia confusa is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, specifically southeastern Africa from Port Elizabeth to southern Mozambique, Zanzibar and Pemba (5° to 30ºS), and Madagascar.

children.name: Sepia cottoni
children.description: Sepia cottoni is a species of cuttlefish native to the southeastern Indian Ocean, specifically from northwest of Broome (17°31' S) to Armstrong Point, Rottnest Island (32°0' S).

children.name: Sepia dollfusi
children.description: Sepia dollfusi is a species of cuttlefish in the Phylum Mollusca, under the Class Cephalopoda, and under the Order Sepiida.

children.name: Sepia elobyana
children.description: Sepia elobyana — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia elongata
children.description: Sepia elongata is a species of cuttlefish native to the northwestern Indian Ocean, specifically from the Red Sea to Somalia.

children.name: Sepia flagellata
children.description: Sepia flagellata — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia furcata
children.description: Sepia furcata — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia gibba
children.description: Sepia gibba is a species of cuttlefish native to the Red Sea.

children.name: Sepia grahami
children.description: Sepia grahami, also known as Ken's cuttlefish, is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Pacific Ocean, specifically along the New South Wales coastline of Australia.

children.name: Sepia hierredda
children.description: Sepia hierredda, the giant African cuttlefish, is a species of cuttlefish from the family Sepiidae, which was previously considered conspecific with the common cuttlefish Sepia officinalis.

children.name: Sepia hirunda
children.description: Sepia hirunda — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia incerta
children.description: Sepia incerta is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, specifically south and east Africa, from Port Elizabeth to Mozambique (north to 18ºS).

children.name: Sepia insignis
children.description: Sepia insignis is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, specifically South Africa, from the Cape of Good Hope to Natal.

children.name: Sepia irvingi
children.description: Sepia irvingi is a species of cuttlefish native to the southeastern Indian Ocean, specifically western Australia, from Cockburn Sound to North West Shelf ( ).

children.name: Sepia ivanovi
children.description: Sepia ivanovi is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, and throughout southeast Africa, including Kenya, and Mozambique, to the mouth of the Zambezi River.

children.name: Sepia joubini
children.description: Sepia joubini is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, specifically South Africa, off Tugela River Mouth ( ), to Cape Natal, off southern Mozambique (24ºS to 25ºS), and in the Saya-de-Malha Bank.

children.name: Sepia juliebarborarum
children.description: Sepia juliebarborarum — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia kiensis
children.description: Sepia kiensis is a species of cuttlefish native to the Indo-Pacific, specifically the Kai Islands, possibly to Timor and northern Australia.

children.name: Sepia koilados
children.description: Sepia koilados is a species of cuttlefish native to the southeastern Indian Ocean, specifically the North West Shelf in western Australia ( to ).

children.name: Sepia mascarensis
children.description: Sepia mascarensis is a species of cuttlefish native to the western Indian Ocean, specifically Saya-de-Malha Bank ( ), Mascarene Ridge, and Cargados-Carajos Shoals.

children.name: Sepia mikuzi
children.description: Sepia mikuzi — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia mira
children.description: Sepia mira is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Pacific Ocean, specifically from the mouth of Clarence River, New South Wales ( ) to off Wooli ( ) in Australia.

children.name: Sepia mirabilis
children.description: Sepia mirabilis is a species of cuttlefish native to the western Indian Ocean.

children.name: Sepia murrayi
children.description: Sepia murrayi, also known as the frog cuttlefish, is a species of cuttlefish in the family Sepiidae.

children.name: Sepia plana
children.description: Sepia plana is a species of cuttlefish native to the coast of Australia, specifically the North West Shelf ( to ).

children.name: Sepia plathyconchalis
children.description: Sepia plathyconchalis is a species of cuttlefish native to the western Indian Ocean, specifically the Saya-de-Malha Bank, Cargados-Carajos Shoals, and St Brandon Shoals.

children.name: Sepia prabahari
children.description: Sepia prabahari — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia pulchra
children.description: Sepia pulchra is a species of cuttlefish native to the southeastern Atlantic Ocean, specifically off the Cape Peninsula in South Africa.

children.name: Sepia reesi
children.description: Sepia reesi is a species of cuttlefish native to the southeastern Indian Ocean.

children.name: Sepia rhoda
children.description: Sepia rhoda is a species of cuttlefish native to the Indo-Pacific, specifically from the Arafura Sea ( ) to the North West Shelf ( ), both off Australia.

children.name: Sepia rouxill
children.description: Sepia rouxill — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia savignyi
children.description: Sepia savignyi — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia saya
children.description: Sepia saya is a species of cuttlefish known only from its type locality in the southwestern Indian Ocean.

children.name: Sepia senta
children.description: Sepia senta is a species of cuttlefish native to the southeastern Indian Ocean, specifically the North West Shelf in Western Australia ( to ).

children.name: Sepia sewelli
children.description: Sepia sewelli is a species of cuttlefish native to the western Indian Ocean, from Cape Guardafui, Somalia ( to ) to Zanzibar and probably Madagascar.

children.name: Sepia simoniana
children.description: thumb | right Sepia simoniana is a species of cuttlefish native to the western Indian Ocean.

children.name: Sepia sokotriensis
children.description: Sepia sokotriensis is a species of cuttlefish native to the western Indian Ocean, specifically off Sokotra Island, and probably east Africa.

children.name: Sepia spec
children.description: Sepia spec — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia subplana
children.description: Sepia subplana is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Pacific Ocean.

children.name: Sepia subtenuipes
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = | status = DD | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Sepia | parent = Sepia (Doratosepion) | species = subtenuipes | authority = & Horikawa, 1987 }}.

children.name: Sepia sulcata
children.description: Sepia sulcata — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia tala
children.description: Sepia tala is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, specifically southwestern Madagascar off Cape Tala.

children.name: Sepia tanybracheia
children.description: Sepia tanybracheia is a species of cuttlefish native to the southeastern Indian Ocean.

children.name: Sepia thurstoni
children.description: Sepia thurstoni is a species of cuttlefish native to the Indian Ocean, specifically the waters off Chennai in India and off Negombo and Hambantota in Sri Lanka.

children.name: Sepia tuberculata
children.description: Sepia tuberculata is a species of cuttlefish native to South African waters from Melkbosstrand (an area near Cape Town) ( ) to Knysna (In the South) ( ).

children.name: Sepia vecchioni
children.description: Sepia vecchioni — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia vercoi
children.description: Sepia vercoi is a species of cuttlefish native to the southeastern Indian Ocean, specifically the waters off Western Australia ( to ).

children.name: Sepia vermiculata
children.description: Sepia vermicularis is a species of cuttlefish endemic to southern Africa.

children.name: Sepia vietnamica
children.description: Sepia vietnamica — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia whitleyana
children.description: Sepia whitleyana — a species in the genus Sepia, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Sepia zanzibarica
children.description: Sepia zanzibarica, or the Zanzibar cuttlefish, is a species of cuttlefish native to the Indian Ocean.

children.name: Acanthosepion
children.description: Acanthosepion — a genus of cuttlefish in the family Sepiidae.

children.name: Acanthosepion ellipticum
children.description: Acanthosepion ellipticum — a species in the genus Acanthosepion, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Acanthosepion whitleyanum
children.description: Acanthosepion whitleyanum — a species in the genus Acanthosepion, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.

children.name: Hemisepius
children.description: Hemisepius — a genus of cuttlefish in the family Sepiidae.

children.name: Hemisepius typicus
children.description: Hemisepius typicus is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean and southeastern Atlantic Ocean.

children.name: Sepiella
children.description: Sepiella — a genus of cuttlefish in the family Sepiidae.

children.name: Sepiella cyanea
children.description: Sepiella cyanea is a species of cuttlefish native to the southwestern Indian Ocean, from Port Elizabeth and Durban north to central Mozambique (26ºN) and Madagascar.

children.name: Sepiella inermis
children.description: Sepiella inermis is a species of cuttlefish in the family Sepiidae.

children.name: Sepiella japonica
children.description: Sepiella japonica, the Japanese spineless cuttlefish, is a species of cuttlefish native to the western Pacific, around China, Japan, and South Korea.

children.name: Sepiella mangkangunga
children.description: Sepiella mangkangunga is a species of cuttlefish native to the Indo-Pacific, specifically off the Northern Territory in Australia ( to ).

children.name: Sepiella ocellata
children.description: Sepiella ocellata is a species of cuttlefish known only from the type locality off Java.

children.description: Sepiella weberi — a species in the genus Sepiella, family Sepiidae. Also known as cuttlefish.
